steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Data set B is formed by subtracting 11 from every $y$-value in data set A. The question asks for the $y$-intercept of the line of best fit for data set B.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content: Subtracting a constant from every $y$-value shifts the entire line of best fit straight down by that constant — the slope stays the same, only the $y$-intercept changes. Read the $y$-intercept of the line of best fit for data set A from the scatterplot: extending it to $x=0$ gives approximately $53.75$. Subtract the shift:$$b_B=53.75-11=42.75$$That matches choice C — C is correct.
